calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-29-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Continued To Feb-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of David Bringas
Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIK HARPER, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm (Tel: 213-381-9988) for Plaintiffs;
Atty. MARK PRAGER, Esq. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'meara LLP (Tel: 510-540-4881) for Defendant David Bringas;
Atty. PEGAH SHETABI, Esq. of Tyson & Mendes LLP (Tel: 628-253-5070) for Defendants
Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, and Rasier-CA, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LARRY JOHNSON's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of David Bringas.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The judge pro tem and parties authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The Motion to Compel is granted to the following extent: Defendant Bringas shall appear for continued deposition within thirty days following the date of order hereon pursuant to notice by Moving Party pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sec. 2025.010 et seq. The Court finds that this motion arises from an unnecessary dispute over the phrasing of deposition questions and repeated objections thereto which should have been resolved by avoiding unnecessary colloquy during the deposition, by rephrasing the deposition questions without assuming a characterization of the relationship between Defendant Uber and Bringas, by agreeing to continuing objections, or by further agreement of counsel. Sanctions are denied because the same would be unjust under the circumstances.

Mr. Harper is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the judge pro tem for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Colin M. Jones And Erik Harper Esq.

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIK HARPER, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm (Tel: 213-381-9988) for Plaintiffs;
Atty. MARK PRAGER, Esq. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'meara LLP (Tel: 510-540-4881) for Defendant David Bringas;
Atty. PEGAH SHETABI, Esq. of Tyson & Mendes LLP (Tel: 628-253-5070) for Defendants
Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, and Rasier-CA, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LARRY JOHNSON's Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The judge pro tem and parties authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The Motion for Protective Order is denied because Court finds that this motion arises from an unnecessary dispute over the phrasing of deposition questions and repeated objections thereto which can be resolved by avoiding unnecessary colloquy during the deposition, by rephrasing the deposition questions without assuming a characterization of the relationship between Defendant Uber and Bringas, by agreeing to continuing objections, or by further agreement of counsel. Sanctions are denied because the same would be unjust under the circumstances.

Mr. Harper is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the judge pro tem for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
